vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded credentials in seed script
vulnerabilities.description: Database seed script contains hardcoded test credentials (email: user1@test.com / user2@test.com with password 'password123') that could be exposed if file is committed to version control or shared.
vulnerabilities.impact: If this seed file is used in production or development, hardcoded credentials could allow unauthorized access
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use environment variables for test credentials and never commit real credentials

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded demo credentials with plain-text passwords
vulnerabilities.description: Demo script contains multiple hardcoded credentials in plain text (alice@test.com, bob@test.com with password 'password123'). This is highly sensitive as it's in archive but still accessible.
vulnerabilities.impact: Anyone with access to the archive can see the demo account credentials
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Move credentials to .env.example or environment variables; remove plain-text passwords from code

vulnerabilities.title: Unvalidated WebSocket message execution
vulnerabilities.description: WebSocket handler executes window.location.reload() based on incoming WS message without proper validation or authentication. Malicious WebSocket messages could trigger uncontrolled page reloads.
vulnerabilities.impact: A compromised or MITM WebSocket connection could reload the page repeatedly, causing denial of service
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Validate WebSocket messages with a secure protocol (signed messages, nonce verification), only accept reload from authenticated sources

vulnerabilities.title: Potential command injection via newArgs array
vulnerabilities.description: While the script uses temp files for body content (good), the newArgs array is joined directly into shell command without proper escaping. If newArgs contains shell metacharacters, injection is possible.
vulnerabilities.impact: Command injection if newArgs contains special characters like ; | & $ backtick
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use array form of execSync instead of string interpolation

vulnerabilities.title: Plain-text key-value storage for sensitive data
vulnerabilities.description: Memory helper stores all data in plain JSON text file at .claude-flow/data/memory.json without encryption. Could contain API keys, tokens, or other secrets.
vulnerabilities.impact: Any sensitive data stored via this helper is exposed as plain text on disk
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Encrypt sensitive values using crypto.secretBox or similar; store only non-sensitive metadata

vulnerabilities.title: Predictable session ID using Date.now()
vulnerabilities.description: Session IDs are generated using Date.now() which is predictable and not cryptographically secure. An attacker could predict future session IDs.
vulnerabilities.impact: Session IDs could be predicted or brute-forced, allowing session hijacking
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use crypto.randomBytes() for session ID generation

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded test password in E2E helper
vulnerabilities.description: E2E test helper contains hardcoded password 'pwd12345' used for all test users.
vulnerabilities.impact: Could leak if test code is run against staging/prod
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use crypto.randomBytes() to generate unique passwords per test run

vulnerabilities.title: Silent failure of temporary file cleanup
vulnerabilities.description: Temporary files created with random names may not be cleaned up if writeFileSync fails, potentially leaving sensitive content on disk.
evidence: Line 35-40: try/finally block with catch-all error handling in cleanup
vulnerabilities.impact: Orphaned temp files could accumulate, potentially containing sensitive content
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Log cleanup failures; consider using os.tmpdir() cleanup utility

vulnerabilities.title: Test data exposed in seed file output
vulnerabilities.description: Seed script prints test credentials to stdout, which gets logged and could be captured by CI/CD systems or terminal history.
vulnerabilities.impact: Credentials logged to stdout become part of CI/CD logs and terminal history
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove console.log of credentials; save to .env.test file instead

vulnerabilities.title: No CSP headers for WebSocket communication
vulnerabilities.description: WebSocket communication is not protected by Content-Security-Policy headers, allowing any page to connect.
evidence: Line 1: WS_URL is derived from window.location.host without validation
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add CSP headers: connect-src 'self' ws: wss: for production deployment

vulnerabilities.title: No validation of task description input
vulnerabilities.description: routeTask() function accepts arbitrary task string without validation or sanitization.
evidence: Line 28: function routeTask(task) { const taskLower = task.toLowerCase();
vulnerabilities.impact: Could be improved with input length validation
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add validation: if (!task || task.length > 1000) throw new Error('Invalid input')

vulnerabilities.title: Demo script uses HTTP instead of HTTPS
vulnerabilities.description: Demo script connects to http://localhost:3000/api which is HTTP.
vulnerabilities.impact: Critical in production - credentials sent in plain text
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use environment variable for BASE_URL; enforce HTTPS in production

summary.criticalFindings: No critical vulnerabilities found, but 4 high-severity issues require immediate attention
mainThreats: Hardcoded credentials in seed and demo scripts (easily leaked)
Command injection vulnerability in GitHub CLI helper
Unencrypted sensitive data storage in memory helper

recommendations.title: Remove all hardcoded credentials
recommendations.action: Replace hardcoded passwords in seed.js and archive/DEMO_SCRIPT.js with environment variables

recommendations.title: Validate WebSocket messages
recommendations.action: Add message signing/verification to scripts/helper.js before executing actions like reload

recommendations.title: Fix command injection in github-safe.js
recommendations.action: Use execSync array form instead of string interpolation for shell safety

recommendations.title: Encrypt sensitive data storage
recommendations.action: Implement encryption for memory.js or restrict to non-sensitive data only

recommendations.title: Use secure session ID generation
recommendations.action: Replace Date.now() with crypto.randomBytes() in session.js

recommendations.title: Add input validation across helpers
recommendations.action: Validate all user inputs in router.js and other helper functions

recommendations.title: Secure WebSocket communication
recommendations.action: Implement CSP headers and WS protocol validation for production

recommendations.title: Improve temp file handling
recommendations.action: Better error logging and cleanup for temporary files

auditScope: Helper scripts, seed data, demo code, frontend WebSocket, and test utilities
